title: The Journey from Platform Nine and Three-Quarters

vocabulary.definition: making you feel sad or low
vocabulary.example: Although this was an improvement in many ways, it did become a bit depressing after a while.
vocabulary.pageHint: Harry's last month with the Dursleys

vocabulary.definition: left somewhere with no way to get away
vocabulary.example: he was stranded in the middle of a station with a trunk he could hardly lift, a pocket full of wizard money, and a large owl.
vocabulary.pageHint: King's Cross, before finding the platform

vocabulary.definition: to push or bump against someone in a crowd
vocabulary.example: People jostled him on their way to platforms nine and ten.

vocabulary.definition: tall and thin with awkwardly long limbs
vocabulary.example: He was tall, thin, and gangling, with freckles, big hands and feet, and a long nose.

vocabulary.definition: a student given special responsibility at Hogwarts
vocabulary.example: “Oh, are you a prefect, Percy?” said one of the twins, with an air of great surprise.

vocabulary.definition: having small hollows in the cheeks when smiling
vocabulary.example: Around half past twelve there was a great clattering outside in the corridor and a smiling, dimpled woman slid back their door and said, “Anything off the cart, dears?”

vocabulary.definition: a sharp tingling feeling, often from fear
vocabulary.example: He was starting to get a prickle of fear every time You-Know-Who was mentioned.

vocabulary.definition: so shocked that you cannot speak
vocabulary.pageHint: Harry doesn't know any Quidditch teams

vocabulary.definition: speaking in a proud, disapproving way
vocabulary.example: “All right—I only came in here because people outside are behaving very childishly, racing up and down the corridors,” said Hermione in a sniffy voice.

vocabulary.definition: to crowd or fill a place in large numbers
vocabulary.example: They crammed their pockets with the last of the sweets and joined the crowd thronging the corridor.
Harry’s stomach lurched with nerves and Ron, he saw, looked pale under his freckles.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Counts down to September first and must find the hidden platform alone.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: A red-haired boy who becomes Harry's first friend on the Hogwarts Express.

characters.name: Hermione Granger
characters.description: A bossy, know-it-all girl who has already read all the course books.

characters.name: Draco Malfoy
characters.description: The pale boy from Diagon Alley who insults Ron and offers Harry the wrong kind of friendship.

characters.name: Neville Longbottom
characters.description: A round-faced boy who keeps losing his toad, Trevor.

background.hook: Harry's last weeks with the Dursleys are strange and silent. On September first, Uncle Vernon dumps him at King's Cross — but platform nine and three-quarters doesn't seem to exist. Once aboard the Hogwarts Express, Harry meets Ron, Hermione, and an enemy from Diagon Alley.
background.readingFocus: Watch how Harry finds the platform and who he sits with on the train. Notice what the sweets, cards, and conversations teach him about wizard life.

miniCheck.stem: If you are stranded, you are —

options.text: stuck somewhere with no way to leave

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry finds platform nine and three-quarters, makes friends on the train, and arrives at Hogwarts
Harry shops for his wand in Diagon Alley
Hagrid tells Harry about Voldemort in the hut on the rock
Harry spends a month locked in his cupboard

comprehension.explanation: The chapter covers the trip to King's Cross, the train journey with Ron and Hermione, meeting Malfoy, and arriving at the castle.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry get onto platform nine and three-quarters?

options.text: He walks straight at the barrier between platforms nine and ten without stopping
Uncle Vernon shows him a secret door
He taps bricks with his wand
A guard opens a hidden gate for him

comprehension.explanation: Mrs. Weasley tells Harry to walk straight at the barrier; he runs and passes through to the Hogwarts Express.

comprehension.stem: What is the name of Ron's rat?
comprehension.explanation: Ron pulls out a fat gray rat named Scabbers, who hardly ever wakes up.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry feel when Uncle Vernon leaves him at the station?

options.text: His mouth goes dry and he tries hard not to panic
He laughs along with the Dursleys
He is relieved they are finally being kind
He doesn't care because he knows the way

comprehension.explanation: After the Dursleys drive away laughing, Harry's mouth goes dry and he has ten minutes left with no idea what to do.

comprehension.stem: Why is Ron shocked when Harry says Voldemort's name?

options.text: Most wizards are too afraid to say You-Know-Who's name aloud
Saying the name is illegal on the train
Ron has never heard the name before

comprehension.explanation: Ron gasps that Harry said You-Know-Who's name — most people are too frightened to say it.

comprehension.stem: What happens when Ron tries to turn Scabbers yellow?

options.text: Nothing — the spell doesn't work and the rat stays asleep

comprehension.explanation: Ron waves his wand and chants a spell, but Scabbers stays gray and fast asleep.

comprehension.stem: How do first-year students cross the lake to Hogwarts?

options.text: In little boats guided by Hagrid
Through the Forbidden Forest on foot
On the Hogwarts Express straight into the castle

comprehension.explanation: Hagrid calls "No more'n four to a boat!" and the fleet glides across the smooth black lake.

vocabulary.stem: To be stranded means to be —

options.text: stuck somewhere with no way to get away

vocabulary.explanation: Harry is stranded at King's Cross with a heavy trunk and no idea how to reach the platform.

vocabulary.stem: He was tall, thin, and ___, with freckles, big hands and feet, and a long nose.
vocabulary.explanation: Ron is described as tall, thin, and gangling when Mrs. Weasley points him out.

options.text: a Hogwarts student with special duties
a spell to turn rats yellow

vocabulary.explanation: Percy wears a shiny silver badge with a P — he is a prefect with his own compartment.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'dumbfounded'?
vocabulary.explanation: Ron looks dumbfounded when Harry admits he doesn't know any Quidditch teams.

vocabulary.stem: People ___ him on their way to platforms nine and ten.
vocabulary.explanation: Harry walks faster toward the barrier as people jostle him on the way to platforms nine and ten.

cloze.title: The Journey from Platform Nine and Three-Quarters
paragraphs: Harry's last month with the Dursleys was odd — they ignored him, which grew (1) ___ after a while. At King's Cross, Uncle Vernon abandoned him, and Harry was (2) ___ with no idea how to reach platform nine and three-quarters.

Mrs. Weasley told him to run at the barrier. People (3) ___ him, but he broke through and boarded the Hogwarts Express. He met Ron, a (4) ___ red-haired boy, and later a smiling, (5) ___ trolley witch sold them wizard sweets.
Malfoy insulted the Weasleys, but Scabbers bit Goyle and drove them off. As the train neared Hogwarts, Harry's stomach (6) ___ with nerves and students (7) ___ the corridors toward the exit.

gaps.explanation: Being ignored by the Dursleys became a bit depressing after a while.
Harry is stranded in the middle of the station with a trunk he can hardly lift.
People jostle Harry on their way to platforms nine and ten.
Ron is tall, thin, and gangling with freckles and big hands.
A smiling, dimpled woman sells sweets from the food trolley.
Harry's stomach lurched with nerves as they neared Hogwarts.
Harry and Ron join the crowd thronging the corridor.
